dimensionClaims.auditScore: Higher gpt-5.5 audit score = more polished surface that lands as 'senior craft' to a typical engineer. Drew weights this less than designJudgement; tracking it as a guardrail not a target.
dimensionClaims.criticalCount: Zero a11y / contrast / structural critical findings = baseline trust. Non-zero = the page has a real bug a senior designer would not ship.
dimensionClaims.designJudgement: Drew's 0–10 verdict on visual quality, density, alignment, and creative originality. THIS is the metric we evolve against. Drew rates current home as 3/10 with gpt-5.5 at 7.0; designJudgement is what's actually missing.
dimensionClaims.buildPasses: Boolean — does `npm run build` succeed AND every page render 200 OK on the dev server. Hard gate; a variant that doesn't build can't ship regardless of other dimensions.

qualityBar: S-TIER OR BUST — output must withstand review at Linear / Vercel / Stripe / Modal / Replicate / Anthropic / Resend / Cursor / Arc / Brex level. 'Decent improvement' is a fail. Ship a homepage that would not look out of place on those sites' homepages today.

referenceSites.name: Linear
referenceSites.study: type-driven hero, mono accents, depth stack, surgical motion

referenceSites.name: Vercel
referenceSites.study: full-bleed hero with framework logos, dense feature grids, scroll-triggered reveals, edge-network maps

referenceSites.name: Stripe
referenceSites.study: alternating dark/light sections, animated diagrams, customer logo proof, sticky-scroll product narratives

referenceSites.name: Modal
referenceSites.study: playful brand color, dense live-feed dashboards, code-on-left/output-on-right framing, real metrics

referenceSites.name: Replicate
referenceSites.study: model gallery as the hero, real outputs, brand-vivid palette, density-as-feature

referenceSites.name: Anthropic
referenceSites.study: editorial type, single dominant headline, restraint, intentional whitespace

referenceSites.name: Resend
referenceSites.study: code-first hero, DX as identity, terminal aesthetic, sparse motion

referenceSites.name: Arc
referenceSites.study: spatial composition, big imagery, generous whitespace

referenceSites.name: Brex
referenceSites.study: trust scaffolding, customer logos, named testimonials, financial polish
